text: Fill in the blanks about the knee joint, anterior compartment of the leg, and dorsum of the foot.
questions: All four muscles of the anterior compartment of the leg are supplied by the *deep peroneal/deep peroneal nerve/deep fibular* nerve. Injury to this nerve causes *foot drop/footdrop:inability to dorsiflex*.

The anterior tibial artery becomes the *dorsalis pedis/dorsalis pedis artery* artery at the ankle, which is palpated between the tendons of extensor hallucis longus and *extensor digitorum longus/EDL*.

The classic knee injury triad (unhappy triad) involves the *anterior cruciate/ACL* ligament, *medial collateral/MCL/medial collateral ligament* ligament, and *medial meniscus/medial meniscus:C-shaped cartilage*.
The knee is 'unlocked' from full extension by the *popliteus/popliteus:rotates tibia internally* muscle, which internally rotates the tibia to initiate flexion.
